ruleset.name: Go Rules
ruleset.description:
PHPMD's default rules tuned for idiomatic Go. It pulls in every built-in ruleset, but quiets a few rules whose PHP defaults are a poor fit for Go:
* Naming/ShortVariable is dropped: short names (i, r, ok, fn, ...) are idiomatic in Go, where scope is small. * Naming/LongVariable's maximum is raised to 35, since Go favors descriptive MixedCaps names over PHP's terser style. * Design/ExitExpression is dropped because os.Exit in main() is idiomatic. * Design/CountInLoopExpression is dropped because len() on a Go slice is O(1), unlike PHP's count(). * CleanCode/ElseExpression is dropped: `else` is idiomatic in Go. * CleanCode/BooleanArgumentFlag is dropped: boolean parameters are common in Go's own standard library. * UnusedCode/UnusedFormalParameter is dropped: unused parameters are routinely required to satisfy interfaces and standard signatures (e.g. http.HandlerFunc).
These three dropped rules are bundled in the opt-in `opinionated` ruleset for anyone who wants the stricter, more PHP-flavoured style. See opinionated.xml.
